title: [rampardos] Battle Guide
summary: A one-shot physical [rock] wallbreaker built on base 165 Atk and [sheer-force] — [head-smash] loses its recoil and hits like a finisher on contact.
strengths: Base 165 Atk is the top of the physical chart in the format
[sheer-force] nullifies [head-smash] recoil and boosts [zen-headbutt], stone-edge, and [crunch] by 1.3x
Coverage options line up cleanly: [earthquake], [zen-headbutt], and [crunch] cover every common [rock] resist
weaknesses: Base 58 Speed and 60 Defense mean almost every offensive Pokemon outspeeds and one-shots [rampardos]
2x weak to Fighting, [ground], Water, and Grass — priority Fighting shuts it down completely
Without [focus-sash] it rarely gets a second turn

recommendedMoves.reason: is the STAB nuke and [sheer-force] removes its recoil entirely

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[rampardos] exists for exactly one reason: base 165 Atk is the highest physical Attack stat in the format, and [sheer-force] turns [head-smash] into a recoil-free STAB nuke. Base 58 Speed and 60 Defense are frankly terrible, so every game with [rampardos] is about landing exactly one [head-smash] at the right moment and accepting that the trade is often the first plan. You run [rampardos] as a dedicated single-target wallbreaker, not as a sweeper.

## Strengths
Stacked with Jolly and STAB, [sheer-force] [head-smash] is one of the highest single-hit damage numbers available, and because [sheer-force] cancels recoil, [rampardos] can actually double-tap when the situation allows. [earthquake] lets it punch through the Steel, Electric, and [rock]-resist cores that normally wall mono-[rock]. [zen-headbutt] and [crunch] both get the [sheer-force] multiplier too, which means [rampardos] does not lose damage when it has to pivot into a coverage slot instead of the main STAB.

## Weaknesses & Answers
Everything stems from the defensive spread. 97/60/50 bulk means any 2x hit from Fighting, [ground], Water, or Grass is a likely OHKO, and 58 Speed hands initiative to almost every offensive Pokemon in the tier. Priority moves like mach-punch or bullet-punch end [rampardos] outright if [focus-sash] is already broken, and Scarf Fighting users shut the answer window completely.

## Recommended Moveset
[head-smash] is mandatory — everything else in the set is built around the fact that [sheer-force] erases its recoil clause. [earthquake] is the second locked slot, handling the Steel and Poison walls that would otherwise sit on [rock] STAB indefinitely. [zen-headbutt] is the Fighting answer and still benefits from [sheer-force], so it is not just filler coverage. The final slot is [crunch] to punch through Psychics and Ghosts; the alternative is stone-edge for a higher-damage secondary [rock] attack when coverage is less important than nuking.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[focus-sash] is the only correct item. 97/60 defenses make any other holdable effectively a one-turn cushion and sash at least guarantees [head-smash] one full send-off. [sheer-force] is locked and mold-breaker would cost too much damage to be worth considering. For Tera, [rock] is the standard for the extra STAB multiplier on [head-smash], and [ground] is the defensive swap that removes the Fighting weakness while still buffing [earthquake].

## Teammates & Synergy
[rampardos] needs a guaranteed entry turn, so fake-out users and slow pivots are mandatory support — anything that removes an opposing priority threat in advance. A setup sweeper in the back of the team should be ready to capitalize on the pace [head-smash] creates, since [rampardos] itself cannot clean. A [ground]-resistant pivot rounds out the team by soaking the attacks [rampardos] cannot switch into.

## Play Patterns
Do not send [rampardos] in while opposing Fighting or [ground] threats are healthy — wait until the counter list is gone, then drop it in on a predicted wall and fire [head-smash] for a forced KO. With [focus-sash] intact you can get a second turn — usually [earthquake] or [zen-headbutt] — but plan every game around the assumption that [rampardos] only gets one real action before exiting.
